req.title: Reconciliation report
req.statement: The system shall produce a daily reconciliation report comparing internal ledger to correspondent bank; non-zero variance shall open an incident.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-025 defines three decision axes: (1) variance == 0 → report stored, no incident; (2) variance != 0 → report stored + incident opened; (3) variance magnitude drives incident severity. This yields EP partitions (zero vs non-zero variance), a BVA case at the exact zero/non-zero boundary (smallest representable non-zero variance, e.g. 0.01), a DT case covering severity routing logic (large variance → HIGH severity), a NEG case for a missing/malformed EOD trigger payload, and a STC case verifying the state change from 'no incident' to 'incident OPEN' on the first variance. Five tests satisfy ≥ 3 required types (positive, negative, boundary) and cover all heuristics.

tests.name: test_reconcile_zero_variance_no_incident
preconditions: Internal ledger balance equals correspondent bank balance (variance = 0)
EOD cron job credentials are available via <fixture: cron_auth_headers>
actions: POST /cron/reconcile with valid EOD payload where ledger_balance == bank_balance
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No incident record created in the system
trace: R-FIN-025: non-zero variance shall open an incident (contrapositive: zero variance opens none)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ENDPOINT = "/cron/reconcile"

@pytest.fixture
def cron_auth_headers():
    return <fixture: cron_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup_incidents():
    <helper_fn: delete_incidents_for_date>("today")
    yield
    <helper_fn: delete_incidents_for_date>("today")

def test_reconcile_zero_variance_no_incident(cron_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "trigger": "EOD",
        "ledger_balance": "10000.00",
        "bank_balance": "10000.00"
    }
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, json=payload, headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["report_stored"] is True
    assert body["incidents_opened"] == 0
    incidents = <helper_fn: get_incidents_for_date>("today")
    assert len(incidents) == 0

tests.name: test_reconcile_nonzero_variance_opens_incident
preconditions: Internal ledger balance differs from correspondent bank balance by 500.00
EOD cron job credentials are available via <fixture: cron_auth_headers>
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query incident store for today's incidents
expected: Incident record exists with status OPEN and variance 500.00
trace: R-FIN-025: non-zero variance shall open an incident
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ENDPOINT = "/cron/reconcile"

@pytest.fixture
def cron_auth_headers():
    return <fixture: cron_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup_incidents():
    <helper_fn: delete_incidents_for_date>("today")
    yield
    <helper_fn: delete_incidents_for_date>("today")

def test_reconcile_nonzero_variance_opens_incident(cron_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "trigger": "EOD",
        "ledger_balance": "10500.00",
        "bank_balance": "10000.00"
    }
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, json=payload, headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["report_stored"] is True
    assert body["incidents_opened"] >= 1
    incidents = <helper_fn: get_incidents_for_date>("today")
    assert len(incidents) >= 1
    assert incidents[0]["status"] == "OPEN"
    assert float(incidents[0]["variance"]) == pytest.approx(500.00)

tests.name: test_reconcile_minimum_nonzero_variance_boundary
preconditions: Internal ledger balance exceeds bank balance by the minimum currency unit (0.01)
EOD cron job credentials are available via <fixture: cron_auth_headers>
actions: Capture HTTP response status, body, and incident details
expected: Incident has variance 0.01 and status OPEN
trace: R-FIN-025: non-zero variance shall open an incident
R-FIN-025: boundary between zero-variance (no incident) and minimum non-zero variance (incident)

@pytest.fixture

def test_reconcile_minimum_nonzero_variance_boundary(cron_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "trigger": "EOD",
        "ledger_balance": "10000.01",

    }
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, json=payload, headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()

tests.name: test_reconcile_large_variance_high_severity_incident
preconditions: Variance exceeds HIGH severity threshold (e.g. > 10000.00 per business rule)
EOD cron job credentials are available via <fixture: cron_auth_headers>
actions: Capture HTTP response and query incident severity
trace: R-FIN-025: incident severity based on variance
R-FIN-025: non-zero variance shall open an incident
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ENDPOINT = "/cron/reconcile"
HIGH_SEVERITY_THRESHOLD = 10000.00

@pytest.fixture
def cron_auth_headers():
    return <fixture: cron_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup_incidents():
    <helper_fn: delete_incidents_for_date>("today")
    yield
    <helper_fn: delete_incidents_for_date>("today")

def test_reconcile_large_variance_high_severity_incident(cron_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "trigger": "EOD",
        "ledger_balance": "25000.00",
        "bank_balance": "10000.00"
    }
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, json=payload, headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["report_stored"] is True
    incidents = <helper_fn: get_incidents_for_date>("today")
    assert len(incidents) >= 1
    assert incidents[0]["severity"] == "HIGH"
    assert incidents[0]["status"] == "OPEN"
    assert float(incidents[0]["variance"]) > HIGH_SEVERITY_THRESHOLD

tests.name: test_reconcile_incident_state_transition_open_on_variance
preconditions: No open incidents exist for today (clean state)
EOD cron job credentials are available via <fixture: cron_auth_headers>
actions: Assert zero open incidents exist before trigger
Query incident store again and assert state change to OPEN
expected: After trigger: >= 1 incident with status OPEN
Incident transitioned from non-existent to OPEN state
trace: R-FIN-025: non-zero variance shall open an incident
R-FIN-025: state transition — no incident → OPEN incident on variance detection
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ENDPOINT = "/cron/reconcile"

@pytest.fixture
def cron_auth_headers():
    return <fixture: cron_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup_incidents():
    <helper_fn: delete_incidents_for_date>("today")
    yield
    <helper_fn: delete_incidents_for_date>("today")

def test_reconcile_incident_state_transition_open_on_variance(cron_auth_headers):
    pre_incidents = <helper_fn: get_incidents_for_date>("today")
    assert len(pre_incidents) == 0, "Precondition: no open incidents before reconcile"
    payload = {
        "trigger": "EOD",
        "ledger_balance": "10200.00",
        "bank_balance": "10000.00"
    }
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, json=payload, headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["report_stored"] is True
    post_incidents = <helper_fn: get_incidents_for_date>("today")
    assert len(post_incidents) >= 1
    assert all(i["status"] == "OPEN" for i in post_incidents)
